item.title: Deploy preflight should not hard-fail when Ollama is absent for local-model blueprints
item.intent: The e2e smoke test (FEAT-018) revealed that `clawhq up` hard-fails on preflight when Ollama is not installed, even though the Docker build succeeds. In CI environments and on machines without Ollama, the full user journey cannot complete. The preflight Ollama check should warn instead of block, or `--skip-preflight` should be documented as the CI workaround.
item.description: Discovered during FEAT-018 initial e2e run: `clawhq up -d <dir> --token <tok> --skip-firewall` exits 1 with 'Ollama is not installed — required for local model inference'. The blueprint uses local model provider, so preflight rejects deployment. This blocks the full user journey in any environment without Ollama. Options: (a) downgrade Ollama check to a warning when model routing is local, (b) allow --skip-preflight to bypass gracefully, (c) add a --no-ollama flag that switches to a no-model-needed mode for testing.
steps: Review preflight Ollama check in src/build/launcher/ — determine if it should be a hard gate or warning
For local-model blueprints, downgrade Ollama absence to a warning (agent can still start, just no inference)
Ensure --skip-preflight cleanly bypasses all preflight checks including Ollama
Update e2e smoke test to use --skip-preflight for the up step so CI can pass end-to-end
Add test coverage for preflight with and without Ollama
acceptanceCriteria: clawhq up completes (containers start) even when Ollama is not installed, with a clear warning
e2e smoke test passes the 'up' step in CI environments without Ollama
--skip-preflight flag cleanly bypasses the Ollama check
